text: SMILE (SIDBI Make in India Soft Loan Fund for MSMEs) Objective: Provides soft loans to MSMEs for debt-equity ratios during establishment or expansion. Eligibility: Existing and new MSMEs in manufacturing/service sectors, prioritizing 'Make in India' sectors. Benefits: Soft loans with competitive interest rates and tenures up to 10 years (including moratorium). Application: Approach nearest SIDBI branch with business plan and financial statements.

PM Vishwakarma Objective: End-to-end support for artisans in 18 traditional trades. Eligibility: 18+ years old, engaged in specified trades, self-employed, no similar loans in last 5 years. Benefits: Skill training (Rs. 500/day stipend), toolkit incentive (up to Rs. 15,000), collateral-free loans (up to Rs. 3 lakh at 5% interest), and digital transaction incentives. Application: Online via PM Vishwakarma portal (https://pmvishwakarma.gov.in/) or CSCs.

Startup India Initiative Objective: Flagship program to nurture innovation and entrepreneurship. Eligibility: Private Ltd, LLP, or Partnership; <10 years old; turnover <Rs. 100 crore; focus on innovation/improvement. Benefits: DPIIT recognition, tax exemptions (80-IAC), simplified compliance, IPR fast-tracking, and access to public procurement. Application: Online via Startup India portal (https://www.startupindia.gov.in/) for DPIIT recognition.

Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) & Credit Guarantee (CGSS): FFS: Provides capital to AIFs which invest in DPIIT-recognized startups. Access equity funding and mentorship through Daughter Funds. CGSS: Provides collateral-free credit guarantee for loans up to Rs. 10 crore for DPIIT-recognized startups. Application: Approach SIDBI-supported AIFs for FFS, or Member Institutions (Banks/NBFCs) for CGSS.

Atal Innovation Mission (AIM) Objective: Promote innovation culture in schools, universities, and industry. Components: Atal Tinkering Labs (Schools), Atal Incubation Centres (Universities/HEIs), and Atal New India Challenges (Startups). Benefits: Grants up to Rs. 10 crore for incubators or Rs. 1 crore for innovation challenges, plus mentorship. Application: Identify specific program on AIM website (https://aim.gov.in/) and submit proposals during open calls.
